specializations: Sustainable biotechnology for large-volume cosmetic ingredients
product_highlights: Luvitol® Bio — bio-based emollient derived from renewable raw materials via biotransformation
Vitamin C (L-ascorbic acid) via fermentation — large-scale biotechnology production of cosmetic-grade ascorbic acid
Hyacare® — fermentation-derived hyaluronic acid in multiple molecular weight grades for skin care
Bio-based niacinamide — fermentation route delivering pharmaceutical-grade niacinamide for cosmetics
notes: BASF, the world's largest chemical company, operates a significant biotechnology division within its Personal Care segment producing large-volume cosmetic actives via fermentation and enzymatic transformation. BASF's biotech-derived portfolio includes fermentation-produced hyaluronic acid under the Hyacare® brand in multiple molecular weight grades, bio-based vitamins including ascorbic acid and niacinamide, and biotransformation-derived emollients with superior sustainability profiles. BASF's scale and global manufacturing network make it a critical supplier for personal care brands seeking biotech-derived ingredients with consistent quality, regulatory documentation, and long-term supply security.
